title: Your Secret Right to Cash
description: Physical cash historically gave people a private, bearer-style way to pay without routine permission checks or transaction surveillance. As money became digital and intermediated, that default right weakened, making privacy and censorship resistance explicit design goals rather than ambient properties.

resources.title: Your Secret Right to Cash
resources.notes: Peter Van Valkenburgh's essay on why private bearer cash mattered by default and what is lost when payments become fully intermediated.

resources.title: The Case for Electronic Cash
resources.notes: Extends the essay's argument into digital cash design, privacy, and exclusion risks in mediated payment systems.

resources.title: The Little Bitcoin Book
resources.notes: Human-rights framing for why open access, privacy, and resistance to exclusion matter for money.
